name: Mr. B's Caramels
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 9-inch square pan and set aside.
2. In a heavy saucepan, stir together sugar, corn syrup, honey, butter, salt, and 1 cup of cream until well combined.
3. Heat over medium-low heat until the mixture reaches a boil. Continue to boil for 2-3 minutes without stirring.
4. Uncover the pan, then use a pastry brush dipped in cold water to wash down the sides of the pan.
5. Keep boiling and stirring the mixture until it reaches 244°F (firm-ball stage) on a candy thermometer.
6. Slowly add the remaining cup of cream to the saucepan without breaking the boil. Continue boiling and stir until the mixture reaches 244°F (firm-ball stage) again.
7. Remove from heat, then stir in vanilla extract.
8. Pour the caramel into the prepared pan and let it cool until firm.
9. Cut the cooled caramel into 1-inch squares.
10. Wrap each piece individually in waxed paper or plastic wrap.
11. Store the caramels in a cool place. Makes approximately 2 1/2 lbs. Each piece contains 65 calories, 0 grams protein, 11 grams carbohydrates, 2 grams fat, and 8 milligrams of cholesterol (with butter or margarine).
